description:
src/lib.rs:307-315 — the test guards with `if std::env::var("DOPPLER_TOKEN").is_ok() { return; }`, returning a successful (passing) result without making any assertion. A developer who has DOPPLER_TOKEN exported (very likely if they're using this crate) will see GREEN even if the token-missing logic regresses.

Trigger: any developer with DOPPLER_TOKEN exported running `cargo test -p secretx-doppler`.
Bad behavior: false-positive PASS hides regressions in the env-var-not-set error path, which is exactly the path that affects user onboarding (the most common first-run failure mode).

Fix: use std::env::remove_var inside the test (after saving and restoring the prior value), or run the test in a child process with a clean env, or skip-with-warning instead of skip-as-pass. Pattern is duplicated across other backends in this workspace; fix locally and consider lifting to a workspace test helper.
title: from_uri_missing_token test silently passes when DOPPLER_TOKEN is set in env
